assertions.title: Canonical manual journal surface remains singular

steps.action: Run the targeted accounting proof pack from erp-domain with the mission command.
steps.expected: All manual-journal proofs pass and the suite reports a single canonical writer.
steps.observed: BUILD SUCCESS; 136 tests passed. CR_ManualJournalSafetyTest and AccountingControllerJournalEndpointsTest both passed inside the pack.

steps.action: Inspect AccountingController and openapi.json for manual-journal routes.
steps.expected: Only POST /api/v1/accounting/journal-entries is the manual-journal write surface.
steps.observed: Controller exposes createJournalEntry at POST /api/v1/accounting/journal-entries; OpenAPI lists only GET and POST on that path, with POST bound to JournalEntryRequest and no second manual writer.

steps.expected: Client reference is used as idempotency surface, reserved system namespaces are rejected, and concurrent repeats converge on one journal id.
steps.observed: CR_ManualJournalSafetyTest asserts only createJournalEntry is present, concurrent manual journal requests return the same id, and failed retries leave no reserved mapping behind.

commands: ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) && cd "$ROOT/erp-domain" && MIGRATION_SET=v2 mvn -Djacoco.skip=true -Dtest='JournalEntryE2ETest,AccountingControllerJournalEndpointsTest,AccountingControllerIdempotencyHeaderParityTest,CriticalAccountingAxesIT,TS_RuntimeAccountingReplayConflictExecutableCoverageTest,TS_RuntimeAccountingPayrollPostingExecutableCoverageTest,CR_ManualJournalSafetyTest,CR_DealerReceiptSettlementAuditTrailTest,CR_PayrollIdempotencyConcurrencyTest,NumberSequenceServiceIntegrationTest,ReferenceNumberServiceTest,TS_RuntimeReferenceNumberServiceExecutableCoverageTest,OrderNumberServiceTest,InvoiceNumberServiceTest,AccountingServiceTest#dealerReceiptService_routesLiveReceiptFlowThroughJournalEntryService+creditDebitNoteService_routesLiveCreditNoteFlowThroughJournalEntryService+inventoryAccountingService_routesLiveLandedCostFlowThroughJournalEntryService' test
evidence.output: BUILD SUCCESS; Tests run: 136, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0

assertions.title: Receipt and settlement header contract stays stable

steps.action: Inspect OpenAPI for accounting receipt/settlement write routes.
steps.expected: Dealer receipt, hybrid receipt, dealer settlement, supplier settlement, and auto-settle routes accept Idempotency-Key only.
steps.observed: All six accounting POST write routes list Idempotency-Key as the only header parameter; no accounting route lists X-Idempotency-Key.

steps.action: Run route-level parity tests for accounting idempotency headers.
steps.expected: Legacy X-Idempotency-Key is rejected and header/body mismatches fail closed.
steps.observed: AccountingControllerIdempotencyHeaderParityTest passed all 18 tests, including legacy-header rejection and body/header mismatch rejection for dealer receipt, hybrid receipt, dealer/supplier settlement, and auto-settle routes.

commands: python3 OpenAPI route scan for accounting receipt/settlement routes
evidence.output: OpenAPI: each accounting write route exposes Idempotency-Key only; targeted parity test class: 18 tests passed

assertions.title: Replay conflicts stay explicit and diagnosable

steps.action: Inspect exception mapping for accounting concurrency failures.
steps.expected: CONCURRENCY_CONFLICT maps to HTTP 409 with structured metadata.
steps.observed: AccountingController routes ErrorCode.CONCURRENCY_CONFLICT to mappedStatus; AccountingApplicationExceptionResponses maps CONC/BUS/DATA errors to HTTP 409 and includes code, message, path, traceId, and details.

steps.expected: Dealer and supplier replay drift surfaces explicit concurrency conflicts with idempotency and partner metadata.
steps.observed: TS_RuntimeAccountingReplayConflictExecutableCoverageTest passed and its helper asserts ErrorCode.CONCURRENCY_CONFLICT plus idempotency-key, partner-type, and partner-id details on replay drift cases.

evidence.output: Replay-conflict executable coverage passed; helper assertions require ErrorCode.CONCURRENCY_CONFLICT and partner/idempotency metadata

assertions.title: Payroll accounting remains on the canonical posting path

steps.action: Inspect payroll posting controller and facade/service path.
steps.expected: Payroll posting flows through the canonical accounting facade/service path and produces PAYROLL-* journals.
steps.observed: AccountingController.recordPayrollPayment delegates to accountingFacade.recordPayrollPayment; AccountingFacade delegates to accountingService.recordPayrollPayment; PayrollAccountingService builds a PAYROLL source reference and standard journal request.

steps.action: Run payroll posting and payroll concurrency coverage.
steps.expected: Concurrent payroll requests converge on one journal and mismatched replays fail closed.
steps.observed: TS_RuntimeAccountingPayrollPostingExecutableCoverageTest passed; CR_PayrollIdempotencyConcurrencyTest passed inside the targeted pack and exercises payroll run idempotency, canonical PAYROLL-* journal creation, and concurrency convergence.

commands: Read/grep inspection of AccountingController.java, AccountingFacade.java, and PayrollAccountingService.java
TS_RuntimeAccountingPayrollPostingExecutableCoverageTest and CR_PayrollIdempotencyConcurrencyTest via targeted accounting proof pack
evidence.output: Payroll posting coverage passed within the 136-test accounting proof pack

frictions.description: The targeted Maven proof pack recompiles a broad test set and emits noisy but benign Java agent and accounting cache warnings during execution.
frictions.resolution: Used the single targeted accounting proof pack as the authoritative signal and recorded the final surefire summary only.

summary: Validated all 4 assigned accounting-core write-contract assertions: manual journal surface singular, accounting header contract stable, replay conflicts explicit with metadata, and payroll posting stays on the canonical PAYROLL path.
